tagline: A weekly observatory of trends in the indexed linguistics and language-education literature.

openalex._comment: Both linguistics subfields are unioned on purpose. 1203 (Arts and Humanities) is ~5x larger than 3310 (Social Sciences); scoping to 3310 alone would silently drop ~83% of the literature. Counts verified against the live OpenAlex /subfields endpoint on the date above; re-verify at build time.

trends._comment: Guards against fake trends: a topic must clear min_works_per_window and persist across persistence_snapshots consecutive runs before it is labelled rising/falling. The trailing weeks and the first months after launch are shown as provisional.

coverage_note: This site measures the OpenAlex-indexed, mostly-English, journal-article slice of the field. It under-represents books and edited volumes, conference proceedings, grey literature, and non-English scholarship, all of which carry real theoretical weight in applied linguistics. Read these as trends in the indexed journal literature, not a complete picture of the field.
